207044.explanation: Together you are stronger. The world is bigger than our municipality. We will and must make use of this if we want to be able to make progress. By collaborating, we are stronger in our province and towards The Hague.

207045.explanation: Neighborhood platforms are already regarded as partners of the municipality with all the associated subsidies and other financial compensations. We find it unnecessary to allocate extra budget for this again.

207046.explanation: We already have budgets and partners within the municipality for this as well. Of course, initiatives may arise that are not yet part of this, in which case tailored solutions are possible.

207047.explanation: Not necessarily. In any case, we find it very important that our tourist attractions actually see benefits from the taxes we levy. In short: the money must truly be spent on our tourism and not on peripheral matters.

207049.explanation: At this moment, we see that there are more than enough companies established here or wanting to establish themselves. We are and will remain an attractive location for entrepreneurs in any case. No extra money needs to be directed towards this.

207050.explanation: First, we must optimize and improve our existing sites where possible. Only after that should we consider any expansion and whether it is even desirable. We are more or less 'full'.

207051.explanation: Opening hours are regulated and determined by law. In our view, it is unwise for operators to decide this themselves for the livability of our centers and neighborhoods. This should always be done in consultation with other stakeholders.

207053.explanation: Agricultural land is not sacred, nor is nature reserve land. We believe a healthy balance of interests should take priority. Each project or development must be assessed on its usefulness and necessity before determining what it should become.

207054.explanation: Residents who want to take action together or individually must be able to count on support from the municipality. This does not always have to be financial; knowledge and support can also be shared.

207055.explanation: There is already national policy for this goal. If we simply adhere to it, there is in principle no need to agree on it again at the municipal level. No need to introduce more rules that are basically redundant.

207056.explanation: Against. If we want our energy costs to remain affordable, now and in the future, it is good that the municipality helps people achieve this. We must certainly continue to offer this helping hand to convince people to do so.

207057.explanation: This is not realistic, at least not without drastically increasing the burdens on residents in other ways. We are therefore firmly opposed and repeat our motto; the polluter must pay.

207059.explanation: Completely agree. Nature-friendly mowing can be done in certain places, but this can and must never come at the expense of, for example, (traffic) safety.

207061.explanation: We absolutely do not support large solar fields from investors in our municipality. Enough is enough. Of course, small private individuals with a piece of land should be able to place some panels for home use.

207063.explanation: We are not always in favor of heat networks. The location, target group, and investments of residents determine if and how heat networks should be implemented. We choose realism over idealism here.

207064.explanation: No, that is a very unwise move at this moment. We much prefer to possibly find a solution together with the private recycling centers.

207065.explanation: We absolutely disagree. Rewarding good behavior (i.e., separating your waste) is very important. If we stop doing this, we will not reduce the waste stream at all, which will cost our society much more money.

207066.explanation: There is already policy for this, and in our view, it works well. If it turns out we reach fewer people than we want, one can still decide to have the municipality do more for integration.

207067.explanation: Everyone has the right to decent housing, including labor migrants. The municipality may and should actively monitor this in our opinion.

207068.explanation: If the municipality can play a role in helping people find a job, we should always try. Participation in society is very important, and a job can be extremely helpful in that.

207070.explanation: Distributing refugees over our neighborhoods in small-scale shelters is desirable. We only hope it will be feasible. If we get to choose, from 2027 onwards, we opt for small scale.

207071.explanation: People who need it can already turn to the municipality for support. The participation scheme was created for this. If it turns out that even that scheme is insufficient or that certain target groups still fall through the cracks, we believe the municipality should be able and allowed to provide tailor-made solutions.

207072.explanation: Every child must have the chance to develop and grow. We believe the municipality can and should contribute to this. We would like this to be arranged based on income. Strong shoulders can carry more.

207074.explanation: We are in favor of a hockey club in Huissen if there is support and need for it. However, we would like it to be realized near or adjacent to the already existing sports facilities within Huissen.

207075.explanation: We see that police capacity is under pressure in our municipality. Where the police withdraw, other enforcement officers will have to step in. We therefore think more municipal enforcement officers should be appointed who also conduct checks at other times.

207076.explanation: Cameras provide a feeling of safety and can be crucial in identifying witnesses and perpetrators of incidents. For us, there is no problem if camera surveillance is implemented.

207077.explanation: We do not think we should judge this. We believe it is up to the enforcement officers themselves to indicate if there is a need for it. We support the choices they make in this regard.

207080.explanation: Starters find it very difficult to enter the housing market. The municipality can and must play an important role here, so we also think this fund should be increased. It is a loan, not a gift.

207081.explanation: Setting a strict standard is not wise in our view. We should look more broadly than per construction project. What is needed? How do we keep neighborhoods and villages in a good balance? We are perfectly capable of doing that ourselves. A new standard is therefore unnecessary.

207082.explanation: We should impose no restrictions on options to address our housing shortage. Where possible and appropriate, building higher than 4 stories should of course be allowed.

207083.explanation: We want to prevent slumlords from abusing the housing shortage. So, in principle, we want people to live in the house they bought themselves. But we also understand that some private individuals contribute well to the market by renting out their homes.

207085.explanation: Green space is important for relaxation and a healthy living environment. But isn’t our beautiful polder green as well? Does it necessarily require a park? Greenery in our streets can also contribute. We are neither for nor against this.

207086.explanation: Besides the fact that we literally and figuratively already have (too) little space for spatial developments, the average construction period for a nuclear power plant is 25 years. Therefore, we see no short- or medium-term solution for our energy problem here.

207087.explanation: That does not seem wise to us. We must continue to support people with the lowest incomes in providing for their daily needs given inflation and rising costs of basic needs. Together, we take care of each other.

207088.explanation: That is not necessary. Everything is already being done to help this target group. The results have been limited. With our current efforts, we can continue to support this group.

207089.explanation: That does not seem wise to us. We must continue to support people with the lowest incomes in providing for their daily needs given inflation and rising costs of basic needs.

207090.explanation: Especially people with lower incomes have problems financing home insulation. The municipality can and must help this group. This is a gain for our entire society in the long term.

207091.explanation: We are in favor of this. We understand the tension with regular parking as well. Charging spots should primarily be for charging, but where needed, in certain places, they may also be used for regular parking.

207092.explanation: Introducing even more blue zones goes against the principle of hospitable centers. People must be able to park decently and for longer periods in the centers. This is good for our local entrepreneurs and shops.

207093.explanation: There are already initiatives such as the village car where volunteers transport people from one neighborhood to another. We hope this is sufficient. If not, we can support other initiatives to assist with this.

207094.explanation: The dikes must remain accessible to all road users—from walkers to motorcyclists and drivers. But this also means we want to safely design the dikes, with cars as guests.

207095.explanation: Destination traffic must remain possible. The road through Huissen remains an important axis for the entire Lingewaard. However, we must take measures to limit through freight traffic that are feasible and affordable. Destination traffic must remain possible.

207096.explanation: A bypass around Haalderen is, in our opinion, also a solution for traffic developments and related challenges. This also requires commitment from the province and other parties. The bypass is, in our view, a possible long-term solution.

207097.explanation: The idea of a bypass around Huissen is fine, but let's be realistic and acknowledge that this requires patience and significant funds and resources. It is clearly a long-term solution.

207098.explanation: More is not necessarily better. We would rather see the current bicycle paths receive a significant upgrade, better maintenance, widening, and more illuminated bike paths, which can already improve things a lot! A good example, in our view, is the Lingepad. But if more bicycle paths need to be and can be built, that is no problem for us!

207099.explanation: As far as we are concerned, existing legislation already facilitates this in, among others, public buildings and government buildings. Separate regulations from the municipality for entrepreneurs are, in our opinion, unnecessary.

207100.explanation: This policy is established at the national level. In our opinion, it is therefore unnecessary to establish local, municipal policy on this as well.

207101.explanation: The increasing hardening of society causes more and more groups to feel uncomfortable. We must continue to pay attention to this. Not only with words, but also with actions.

207103.explanation: We are not in favor of this. The street coaches often detect problems concerning youth earlier than other agencies and form an important lifeline. Something that allows us, as a municipality, to intervene earlier. So let them stay!

207104.explanation: Good example leads to good follow-up. So let's all try to pursue a healthier lifestyle. That can do no harm, not now and not in the future. Let's continue to make use of our beautiful environment to keep encouraging this.

207105.explanation: Due to the increasing aging population, it will be increasingly necessary to continue facilitating this. It is not only about money, but also about municipal support for the many volunteers who already do this.

207044.explanation: We are in favor of cooperating with other municipalities, especially when it saves costs. However, we want to be able to maintain our own course well.

207045.explanation: Existing budgets can be maintained. It has become clear in recent years that this is sufficient.

207046.explanation: The knowledge in the field of maintenance within the municipality is at a required level. So under the municipality’s supervision, this work should continue to be carried out.

207047.explanation: Lingewaard already applies a low tourist tax rate compared to other municipalities.

207049.explanation: The current policy and budget are sufficient. The municipality is already considered an attractive municipality.

207050.explanation: First further develop and improve existing sites.

207051.explanation: Only for opening during nighttime hours must an exemption be requested.

207053.explanation: Lingewaard.NU-B06 is for the preservation of agricultural land. In our view, the municipality has no role in this.

207054.explanation: Maintain the current scheme for supporting initiatives in the field of sustainable energy, provided their positive effect has been demonstrated.

207055.explanation: Companies with good sustainability objectives are welcome. Currently, we see making fully sustainable operations a requirement as a step too far.

207056.explanation: Support for making homes more sustainable is a good thing, provided that demonstrably positive results are achieved.

207059.explanation: Not immediately necessary. The current level is sufficient.

207061.explanation: Good agricultural land should be used for its intended purpose. Solar panels on roofs.

207063.explanation: The development of heat networks should, as much as possible, be left to market parties. The municipality can co-finance the establishment of heat networks in Lingewaard on the condition that this is financially responsible and low-risk. The objective: to keep the affordability of energy from a heat network for residents at a level comparable to other energy sources.

207064.explanation: Existing market knowledge should be used. The municipality should not take everything upon itself. Shorter distances to dispose of waste encourage residents and yield better results. Of course, all according to applicable environmental standards.

207065.explanation: The polluter must pay for their waste.

207066.explanation: The municipality can be more active in guiding newcomers with a residence status to a job or societal activities.

207067.explanation: Labor migrants must be housed decently. Control over this is necessary.

207068.explanation: The municipality can be more active in guiding newcomers with a residence status to a job.

207070.explanation: From 2028 onwards, other municipalities that have not yet accommodated asylum seekers should be given priority. Stricter national policy, less inflow, and less required reception.

207071.explanation: School swimming is practically not feasible or integrable into the program for all primary schools.

207072.explanation: Music is part of the artistic orientation curriculum in primary schools.

207074.explanation: Hockey is a growing sport. In Lingewaard, hockey is only played in the core of Bemmel. They currently have about 800 members. A second hockey club in Huissen is an option, provided a fully-fledged association with enough aspiring members officially registered comes forward.

207075.explanation: If necessary to better guarantee safety, the number of enforcement officers (boa's) can be increased.

207076.explanation: At locations where many people gather and unrest occurs more frequently, installing cameras provides increased safety.

207077.explanation: A body camera increases the safety of the enforcement officer (boa).

207080.explanation: Maintain the current starter loan and continue to grant it as needed.

207081.explanation: This promotes an increase in the number of affordable homes in Lingewaard.

207082.explanation: The building space within Lingewaard is becoming more limited. High-rise buildings with 5-7 floors, depending on the location, offer solutions to still create sufficient living space.

207083.explanation: A mandatory owner-occupancy requirement contributes to less housing speculation.

207085.explanation: The municipality having to ensure this is realized for all homes in the Lingewaard cores goes too far for us. We do agree with the principle that the municipality should strive for this.

207086.explanation: To meet the rising energy demand in the future, initiating research into the possibilities for a small nuclear reactor (Small Modular Reactor) in or near Lingewaard is desirable.

207087.explanation: People with a low income who cannot make ends meet should be guided and financially supported to avoid getting into trouble.

207088.explanation: Encouraging people to contribute to society can be better supported and promoted by the municipality.

207089.explanation: People with a low income who cannot make ends meet should be guided and financially supported to avoid getting into trouble.

207090.explanation: Residents of Lingewaard with a low income can already make use of the existing scheme to apply for subsidies to insulate their homes.

207091.explanation: The installation of charging stations should be left to the market. As a municipality, we determine where they can be placed, provided that it does not come at the expense of existing parking spaces.

207092.explanation: The existing blue zones are currently fine.

207093.explanation: This is an activity that lies with the public transport provider.

207094.explanation: To better ensure the safety of cyclists on the dikes, this is a starting point. Car traffic cannot be completely prohibited because in some cases the dikes contribute to the accessibility of the town centers.

207095.explanation: A policy must be developed whereby in the long term a ring road can solve the problem for trucks and the heavy car traffic through Huissen. In the short term, measures must be taken to reduce speed through Huissen.

207096.explanation: To achieve a structurally safe solution for accessing Haalderen, a northern ring road is a requirement.

207097.explanation: Access to Huissen requires a structural, well-functioning traffic solution. The current road network is insufficient with all already planned construction projects still ahead. Realizing a ring road on the east side offers a solution for this.

207098.explanation: A good cycling network within the municipality of Lingewaard is a must to encourage cycling and also boost attractiveness for tourists.

207099.explanation: This is primarily the responsibility of the entrepreneur themselves.

207100.explanation: Large rent increases by housing corporations (well above inflation correction) have too much impact on tenants. The municipality needs to be alert to this and stand up for the tenants.

207101.explanation: Subsidies for individual projects can always be applied for and will be assessed based on existing policy to determine eligibility.

207103.explanation: Having street coaches contributes to maintaining or improving contact between young people and the municipality.

207104.explanation: This role mainly lies with the national government. The current efforts from the municipality are sufficient.

207105.explanation: Realizing day care in all villages is a must. This may mean that it requires more money from the municipality to achieve this.

207044.explanation: By working smartly together with other municipalities, the government becomes more efficient. This way we keep control over the municipality's expenses, keeping the financial burden for residents low.

207045.explanation: Neighborhood platforms are important for involvement in the neighborhood and deserve support. Additional structural tax funding is not currently needed. But with good plans, we are happy to collaborate, especially if residents show initiative themselves.

207046.explanation: Residents who maintain the greenery in their neighborhood themselves make a valuable contribution to the living environment. As VVD Lingewaard, we support this. However, this does not always have to be accompanied by financial compensation.

207047.explanation: The tourist tax is invested in making Lingewaard more attractive to tourists. This way, the money benefits both the living environment and the tourism sector. The current rate is appropriate for this. This rate is currently as low as possible and does not need to be further reduced.

207049.explanation: The prosperity of Lingewaard depends on a strong and growing economy. Businesses are indispensable for employment and amenities, whereby expansion of industrial areas must be carefully weighed.

207050.explanation: At the moment, there are still locations in industrial areas that are unoccupied. Therefore, expansion is not necessary now. Existing space must first be utilized.

207051.explanation: During the day, hospitality entrepreneurs must be able to decide for themselves when they are open, except for nighttime opening hours.

207053.explanation: We only allow the conversion of agricultural land into nature reserves on a voluntary basis. This must never be done under duress.

207054.explanation: The generation of sustainable energy should be stimulated via subsidies and support, not through obligations. This preserves support and freedom of choice.

207055.explanation: Within the legal frameworks there is room for every type of business. Entrepreneurs must be able to decide themselves how to make their operations more sustainable, without additional municipal regulations.

207056.explanation: Residents who take the initiative to make their homes more sustainable deserve support. It is good that the portal remains available and that the necessary expertise remains accessible within the municipality.

207057.explanation: Nothing is free! In practice, the costs are still charged to residents through taxes. At VVD Lingewaard, the polluter pays: those who produce the most waste pay the most.

207059.explanation: The current maintenance level of public spaces is sufficient. More maintenance means higher costs and ultimately higher taxes for residents.

207061.explanation: Solar panels on agricultural land have a major impact on the landscape and produce relatively little sustainable energy within the total energy task. Therefore, this is not a good way to generate sustainable energy.

207063.explanation: The installation and financing of heat networks is not a core task of the municipality. The municipality coordinates where necessary but leaves investments and execution to the market.

207064.explanation: A municipal waste collection center costs the community at least one million euros per year. Currently, entrepreneurs fulfill this task adequately. Therefore, it is unacceptable to spend extra tax money on this.

207065.explanation: The more waste there is, the higher the costs for the community. It is logical that households with more waste pay more. This encourages waste reduction and cost-conscious behavior.

207066.explanation: The integration of asylum seekers is not a municipal task but a responsibility of the Central Agency for the Reception of Asylum Seekers (COA).

207067.explanation: Entrepreneurs who employ migrant workers are responsible for proper housing. The municipality must supervise this to prevent abuses and exploitation.

207068.explanation: There should be no distinction between Dutch citizens and status holders when searching for work. Everyone deserves equal opportunities in the labor market.

207070.explanation: Mandatory distribution increases resistance in the communities. Centralized reception offers more control, better guidance, and more efficient deployment of emergency services.

207071.explanation: The current way swimming lessons are organized is sufficient. There is no need for an additional financial contribution from the municipality.

207072.explanation: Associations receive subsidies for youth members. We make no distinction between sports, music, or scouting associations. Everyone is treated equally.

207074.explanation: There is a lot of interest in a hockey club in Huissen. The municipality must realize this in cooperation with the Huissen hockey club and the residents of Huissen.

207075.explanation: The VVD stands for safety. The safety of residents must always be guaranteed in Lingewaard. Police deployment is decreasing in Lingewaard. Therefore, we deploy community enforcement officers (boa’s) for our safety.

207076.explanation: Camera surveillance can make a huge contribution to safety in Lingewaard, especially in high-risk areas. Privacy rules for residents must also be respected.

207077.explanation: Bodycams record incidents objectively and increase the safety of emergency responders and residents.

207080.explanation: Starter loans help young people buy their first home. Owning a home is always an investment in yourself. VVD Lingewaard supports home ownership and starter loans.

207081.explanation: Most residents want to buy a home, not rent. VVD Lingewaard focuses on realizing affordable homes for purchase. With home ownership, you invest in yourself and build your future; renting does not offer this.

207082.explanation: Starters and elderly people have a great need for apartments. Moreover, building apartments promotes movement in the housing market. The height of an apartment building should be tailored and appropriate for the surroundings.

207083.explanation: Investors must be able to create living space for Lingewaard residents. We reject exploitative landlords.

207085.explanation: Imposing more requirements on new construction only makes building new homes more difficult. VVD Lingewaard chooses fewer rules and faster building to offer housing seekers perspective as soon as possible.

207086.explanation: With the current wind turbines, Lingewaard meets its energy demand. A nuclear power plant is unnecessary and would severely damage the landscape.

207087.explanation: There is no reason to spend less money. The current situation is sufficient.

207088.explanation: We support helping the long-term unemployed into paid jobs. We do not consider subsidized jobs a solution.

207089.explanation: Families living in poverty receive benefits from the national government to meet their basic needs, making this policy unnecessary.

207090.explanation: Many subsidies and schemes are already provided by the national government. The municipality does not need to fund this again. The municipality can provide support through the energy information desk.

207091.explanation: Electric driving is the future. Therefore, charging infrastructure must grow with demand, so that electric driving remains a realistic alternative.

207092.explanation: In Bemmel and Huissen there must be sufficient permanent parking space for residents, employees, and visitors. Expanding blue zones increases parking pressure in surrounding residential neighborhoods.

207093.explanation: Solutions such as buses or taxis already exist for transport between villages. Public transport is the responsibility of the province, not the municipality.

207094.explanation: Everyone should be able to use the dike, with mutual consideration. This creates a more attractive dike and more space for recreation.

207095.explanation: Regarding truck traffic, we want to allow destination traffic through Huissen and have non-destination traffic bypass Huissen to reduce nuisance for residents. We will investigate various measures for this, such as a vignette system.

207096.explanation: A long-term traffic solution is needed for Haalderen. The possibilities for a bypass road should be explored, as well as extra connections to the A15, if feasible and affordable.

207097.explanation: A long-term traffic solution is needed for Huissen. The possibilities for a bypass road should be explored, as well as extra connections to the A15, if feasible and affordable.

207098.explanation: The bicycle is widely used in Lingewaard. Good and safe bike paths therefore deserve ongoing attention and investment, where justified.

207099.explanation: We value the accessibility of businesses for everyone. However, this is the responsibility of the entrepreneur. The municipality does not need to contribute here.

207100.explanation: Housing corporations need financial resources to build new homes. These funds come from rental income. A vote against rent increases is a vote against new construction.

207101.explanation: All projects are treated equally. No specific subsidies are granted for LGBTQIA+ projects.

207103.explanation: Street coaches do important work. Their work makes it possible to engage early with hanging-out youth and address problems without adding extra pressure on police and community enforcement officers.

207104.explanation: VVD Lingewaard opposes paternalism. The VVD stands for freedom and personal responsibility. People know best what is good for them.

207105.explanation: The number of elderly is growing. Investing in day care helps them stay vital and provides pleasant daily activities, contributing to quality of life.

207044.explanation: The municipality of Lingewaard is too small to do everything alone. By cooperating with other municipalities, work can be done more efficiently and more can be achieved together. In addition, burdens are shared and knowledge is combined.

207045.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA finds it important that citizen initiatives receive sufficient support. Currently, the available budgets are adequate annually. We choose customization and flexibility: support for neighborhood platforms, village councils, and other initiatives where needed.

207046.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA finds it important to support residents who maintain green spaces in their neighborhoods. This support does not need to be financial. We mainly choose to facilitate with knowledge, materials, and guidance so initiatives can succeed without structural subsidies.

207047.explanation: The tourist tax has recently been made sufficient to cover costs so that tourism costs are fairly distributed and not borne solely by residents. At GroenLinks-PvdA’s proposal, the increase for camping pitches was limited to keep recreation affordable.

207049.explanation: Companies find Lingewaard attractive due to its favorable location near highways. For new businesses we will introduce a livability test, looking at the impact on the living environment and positive contributions for residents.

207050.explanation: Space in Lingewaard is scarce. We want to primarily use it for housing and green areas. GroenLinks-PvdA chooses a green, sustainable, and healthy Lingewaard, with space for people, nature, and livability. This is our priority.

207051.explanation: Cafés and restaurants contribute to lively and attractive centers. Allowing entrepreneurs to set their own opening hours leaves room for customization. This strengthens livability and coziness for young and old, with respect for the surroundings.

207053.explanation: When agricultural land becomes available, careful consideration must be given to the best purpose: housing, nature, or nature-inclusive and organic farming. GroenLinks-PvdA believes this choice should contribute to a sustainable and livable municipality.

207054.explanation: Collectively generating sustainable energy is an important step in the energy transition. GroenLinks-PvdA wants the municipality to support citizen initiatives by providing advice and, where appropriate, subsidies, such as for neighborhood batteries.

207055.explanation: For new companies, we want to implement a livability test. This will assess sustainability and how a company contributes to the living environment and residents’ well-being. This ensures new business activity fits with a healthy and livable Lingewaard.

207056.explanation: At the Energy Desk, residents receive low-threshold and independent assistance with energy questions and, for example, making their homes more sustainable. It is good that this facility exists, free and accessible to all residents in all villages.

207057.explanation: By making the recycling center free for private individuals, we encourage residents to properly and easily dispose of waste. In a circular crafts center, items are reused or repaired. This way, together we ensure clean streets, a healthy environment, and a sustainable municipality.

207059.explanation: For biodiversity, it is important that lawns and verges are not mowed too often. This gives flowers and insects room. Of course, traffic safety always remains paramount, and mowing will be done where necessary.

207061.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA prefers solar panels on roofs but does not exclude solar panels on the ground. These must be sun-following systems that allow dual use (agriculture and energy generation).

207063.explanation: District heating networks are, like electricity networks, an important municipal task. They help heat homes sustainably. GroenLinks-PvdA believes the municipality should co-finance this and is already actively involved in this in Lingewaard.

207064.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants to work towards a single municipal facility in the form of a circular craft center. This combines the recycling center, thrift shop, and repair services. This way, we stimulate reuse and prevent valuable items from being incinerated.

207065.explanation: Waste costs money. So the less waste, the better. Those who separate their waste better should receive financial benefits for it.

207066.explanation: The sooner newcomers can participate in society, the better it is for everyone. By investing in integration, such as language, work, and social encounters, we ensure more connection, opportunities, and a strong community in Lingewaard.

207067.explanation: Labor migrants also have the right to decent housing. Inspection is (unfortunately) necessary for this.

207068.explanation: The sooner status holders can start working, the better it is for them and for society. By helping them towards paid jobs, we encourage participation, independence, and strengthen the community in Lingewaard.

207070.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA is positively open to small-scale shelters, also distributed across villages, because this can promote integration. However, this can only happen if it is affordable and does not result in disproportionate costs for residents. Therefore, we do not unconditionally choose dispersion.

207071.explanation: Education is increasingly given more responsibilities, requiring good choices. Lingewaard already has a safety net arrangement for low-income parents so that every child can learn to swim.

207072.explanation: Acquaintance with art and culture is important for children's development. GroenLinks-PvdA believes every primary school child should have the chance to receive cultural education, such as music lessons. The Lingewaard cultural platform (Clink!) already plays a valuable role in schools.

207074.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA finds it important that the municipality supports sports initiatives and stimulates cooperation between clubs. The municipality building a full hockey field is not self-evident. A small-scale training facility for youth can be an appropriate first step.

207075.explanation: The number of enforcement officers (boa's) should be proportional to the number of inhabitants in Lingewaard.

207076.explanation: Camera surveillance is an invasion of privacy. It should only be used when necessary.

207077.explanation: Bodycams increase safety for enforcement officers. When they themselves find it desirable, they should be equipped with them.

207080.explanation: For young people, it is already difficult enough to get started on the housing market. Starter loans can really make a difference here. Where the municipality can help make housing possible, it should do so.

207081.explanation: There is a severe shortage of affordable housing, both rental and owner-occupied. Lingewaard is currently lagging behind the desired numbers as established by the province.

207082.explanation: Especially in the larger centers, high-rise buildings are a good option. However, the construction must be appropriate to the surroundings.

207083.explanation: Houses are meant to be lived in, not as investment objects.

207085.explanation: Green spaces are important for people, animals, biodiversity, and the climate. They reduce heat stress, improve air quality, and provide space for social encounters.

207086.explanation: Nuclear power plants are expensive, take a long time to build, and produce radioactive waste that remains dangerous for generations. Therefore, GroenLinks-PvdA chooses sustainable energy sources that can be deployed faster and do not pose lasting risks.

207087.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA invests in equal opportunities. We want to properly support people with low incomes. By bundling and simplifying minimum schemes, we make it easier to get help when needed.

207088.explanation: We continue to invest so that job seekers can participate again as quickly as possible, preferably with paid work and otherwise through volunteering.

207089.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA invests in equal opportunities. For residents with low incomes, exemption from municipal taxes remains important. Where possible, the municipality offers a helping hand, and we simplify and make arrangements more accessible.

207090.explanation: Everyone must be able to participate in sustainability and climate adaptation. Through Operation Insulation, residents can already receive financial support if they have difficulty financing modifications to their homes.

207091.explanation: Lingewaard has a good, demand-driven plan for charging stations. The number of charging spots grows with the number of electric cars in a neighborhood. This ensures sufficient charging facilities without unnecessary placement.

207092.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA finds a good balance important between parking for residents and visitors. Currently, there is no reason to further expand the blue zones in Bemmel and Huissen. We will continue to monitor the situation and adjust if necessary.

207093.explanation: Public transport is a provincial matter. However, the municipality must advocate for good accessibility of the centers and between them. Volunteer transportation or options like Automaatje or the Dorpsauto, in which the municipality can play a role, can also be considered.

207094.explanation: Dikes are not always needed as through roads. Therefore, they can be closed off at strategic points with a barrier. This makes the dikes safer for cyclists and pedestrians.

207095.explanation: There is a good alternative for through freight traffic via Huissen, namely the A325/N325. The current through freight traffic causes much nuisance, air pollution, and unsafe situations. We no longer want this.

207096.explanation: Additional roads rarely reduce traffic congestion, and a ring road is also unaffordable. GroenLinks-PvdA prefers to invest in express bike paths and good public transport as attractive alternatives to the car.

207097.explanation: Additional roads rarely reduce traffic congestion, and a ring road is also unaffordable. GroenLinks-PvdA prefers to invest in express bike paths and good public transport as attractive alternatives to the car. Furthermore, Huissen is already connected to existing highways.

207098.explanation: The only solution for the increasingly congested road network is to offer good alternatives that encourage people to use their cars less often. More and better bike paths are one such alternative.

207099.explanation: Accessibility for people with disabilities is important and largely legally required. Entrepreneurs themselves are responsible for this. The municipality can support by thinking along, stimulating, and sharing good examples.

207100.explanation: The municipality ensures that the housing corporations comply with national standards.

207101.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA supports an inclusive society. Everyone must be able to participate and move freely in public spaces, regardless of religion, cultural background, gender, or orientation. Attention to the LGBTQIA+ community is (unfortunately) still very much needed.

207103.explanation: We want all young people to grow up with opportunities. Street coaches play an important role in this.

207104.explanation: Healthy living fits with a green, social, and healthy municipality. Good information helps people make conscious choices and has a preventive effect. By investing in this, the municipality contributes to well-being, health, and quality of life in Lingewaard.

207105.explanation: Good day care is important, but more money alone is not always the solution. GroenLinks-PvdA mainly wants to invest in local initiatives that support and encourage people to care for each other. This strengthens care and involvement close to home.

207044.explanation: Many issues do not stop at the municipal border. D66 Lingewaard chooses cooperation where necessary and effective, for example in the fields of economy, energy, and care. Regional cooperation strengthens our impact and leads to better solutions.

207045.explanation: Neighborhood and village councils know their surroundings best. D66 Lingewaard wants to professionalize this role with clear agreements, support, and advisory rights. This ensures that plans better align with what matters in the neighborhoods and villages.

207046.explanation: Greening is essential for a healthy living environment. D66 Lingewaard promotes initiatives such as Operation Stone Break and supports residents who want to make their street or neighborhood greener together. This contributes to biodiversity, climate adaptation, and pleasant living.

207047.explanation: D66 wants Lingewaard to be and remain attractive to tourists, with a reasonable tourist tax fitting this goal.

207049.explanation: D66 wants a strong, future-oriented local economy. Targeted promotion of Lingewaard as a location for innovative and sustainable companies can help. This must suit the character of the municipality and focus on quality, not growth for growth's sake.

207050.explanation: D66 Lingewaard wants to allow room for entrepreneurship but takes no position on expanding the number of business parks. The focus is on careful use of space, utilizing existing locations, and preventing unnecessary damage to the landscape and living environment.

207051.explanation: D66 Lingewaard wants lively and attractive centers with space for hospitality and entrepreneurship. Entrepreneurs themselves know best what suits their business and customers. Freedom in opening hours can help, as long as it does not cause nuisance to the surroundings.

207053.explanation: D66 finds it important to use space sparingly and to strengthen the Betuwe landscape. Nature development and biodiversity are necessary for a healthy living environment. Where possible with care, there should be space for nature, balanced with agriculture and mindful of farmers and surroundings.

207054.explanation: D66 Lingewaard believes sustainable energy should be accessible to everyone. Local and joint initiatives, such as sharing solar or wind energy, help residents participate in the energy transition. The municipality can encourage this through support and financial assistance.

207055.explanation: D66 values sustainability and a circular economy and wants to encourage companies to operate sustainably. The focus is on stimulation and cooperation, not exclusion.

207056.explanation: D66 Lingewaard finds it important that residents receive good and accessible support when making their homes more sustainable. Energy desks provide advice on energy saving and sustainability and ensure everyone can participate in the energy transition.

207057.explanation: D66 thinks separating and disposing of waste should be easy but adheres to the polluter pays principle. By linking costs to the amount and type of waste, we encourage waste separation, reuse, and reduction of residual waste. This contributes to a circular economy.

207059.explanation: D66 chooses green management that strengthens biodiversity. Less and smarter mowing leads to more flowers, insects, and healthier nature. This contributes to an attractive landscape and a better living environment for people and animals.

207061.explanation: D66 wants to use space sparingly and protect the Betuwe landscape. The effort focuses on careful considerations, where sustainability, landscape, agriculture, and support are examined together. Custom solutions per location are important.

207063.explanation: Heat networks can be an important alternative to natural gas and help heat homes and buildings more sustainably. By co-investing as a municipality, construction can be accelerated and sustainable heat can remain accessible and affordable for residents.

207064.explanation: D66 thinks waste collection should be affordable, transparent, and well organized. A central municipal recycling center can reduce costs, increase control over waste streams, and improve efficiency, as long as accessibility for residents remains well arranged.

207065.explanation: D66 finds it important to reduce waste and encourage reuse. If everyone pays the same, there's no incentive to produce less residual waste. By considering the amount of waste, we promote better separation and work towards a circular economy.

207066.explanation: Good integration ensures newcomers become independent faster and can participate in society. Investing in language, participation, and guidance prevents long-term problems and strengthens social cohesion in Lingewaard. More funding is naturally only needed if demand increases.

207067.explanation: Good and safe housing is important for everyone. Active enforcement by the municipality prevents abuses, overcrowding, and nuisance in neighborhoods. This protects foreign workers (who we need) and contributes to pleasant living for all residents.

207068.explanation: Work is an important step towards independence and participation in society. By actively guiding status holders towards paid employment, we utilize talents, reduce reliance on support, and strengthen social cohesion in Lingewaard.

207070.explanation: D66 finds it important that shelters are organized carefully and in good consultation with residents. Distribution across villages can contribute to support but is not a goal in itself. Each location must be assessed for suitability and feasibility for the environment.

207071.explanation: Swimming skills are essential for children's safety. By supporting school swimming, we ensure all children, regardless of background or income, learn to swim. This reduces inequality and increases safety in and around water.

207072.explanation: Music education contributes to the broad development of children and stimulates creativity, self-confidence, and talent. By making music lessons financially accessible, all children get equal opportunities to develop culturally, regardless of their home financial situation.

207074.explanation: D66 values sports and exercise and wants associations to receive good support. Whether building a specific hockey field is necessary and feasible depends on need, location, costs, and connection with other sports facilities. This requires careful consideration.

207075.explanation: Visible and approachable enforcement officers contribute to a safe feeling in neighborhoods and village centers. With enough community officers deployed, the municipality can act faster against nuisance and work preventively on safety in the living environment.

207076.explanation: D66 considers safety important but does not see CCTV as a standard solution. In some cases, it can help but always temporarily, purposefully, and with respect for privacy. Prevention, visible enforcement officers, and social safety remain the basis.

207077.explanation: Bodycams can contribute to the safety of enforcement officers and careful actions but are not a goal in themselves. Their use should depend on necessity, proportionality, and privacy. Each situation must be weighed to see whether bodycams actually add value.

207080.explanation: The housing market is difficult for young people to access. With starter loans, the municipality helps young people buy their first home and promotes housing market mobility. This contributes to affordable living and keeps Lingewaard attractive for young residents.

207081.explanation: D66 believes affordable housing should be available to everyone. A solid share of social rental housing in new construction helps low-income home seekers, promotes mixed neighborhoods, and ensures housing market circulation. A minimum target of 30% provides direction and clarity for new projects.

207082.explanation: D66 thinks new housing must fit the environment and contribute to pleasant living. In some villages, higher buildings can help build affordably and save space, while lower buildings suit other places better. The right height requires custom solutions per location.

207083.explanation: D66 naturally wants to keep homes as much as possible available for people who want to live in them themselves. But there are also people who buy a house for their children or as an investment for old age. That should remain possible.

207085.explanation: Green space close to home is important for health, relaxation, and social interaction. By creating parks and green areas within walking distance, the municipality encourages exercise and contributes to a pleasant and healthy living environment for all residents.

207086.explanation: D66 focuses on sustainable energy that suits the scale, safety, and landscape of the municipality. A nuclear power plant is an intrusive facility with significant spatial and safety impacts. This requires careful consideration and is not an automatic choice for a municipality like Lingewaard.

207087.explanation: D66 believes everyone should be able to participate. Good support for people with low incomes prevents bigger problems, increases financial security, and ensures access to sports, culture, and financial assistance. Cutbacks here increase inequality.

207088.explanation: Work offers structure, income, and perspective. By creating more subsidized jobs, we help people with barriers to the labor market participate again. This increases independence, utilizes talents, and prevents long-term reliance on support.

207089.explanation: Debt waiver helps people with low incomes stay financially afloat. It prevents problematic debts and increases financial security. D66 believes support should focus on participation and poverty prevention, not increasing financial pressure.

207090.explanation: Insulation lowers energy costs and improves living comfort. By financially supporting low-income households with insulation, the municipality prevents energy poverty and ensures everyone can participate in the energy transition.

207091.explanation: Electric driving is becoming increasingly important for cleaner transport. By placing enough charging stations, the municipality makes sustainable driving accessible to more residents and supports the transition to lower emissions and cleaner air.

207092.explanation: Blue zones can help reduce parking pressure and keep centers accessible, but expansion is not a goal in itself. D66 opts for tailored solutions per location, considering accessibility, livability, and effects on residents and businesses.

207093.explanation: Tailored public transport improves accessibility between villages, especially for elderly people, youth, and those without a car. With flexible solutions like demand-responsive transport, Lingewaard remains accessible and residents can more easily participate in work, care, and social activities.

207094.explanation: D66 wants safe and pleasant dike roads where cyclists and pedestrians have priority. The principle “cars are guests” fits with traffic safety, recreation, and landscape preservation. This leads to calmer traffic and fewer unsafe situations.

207095.explanation: D66 wants to improve the quality of life and traffic safety in Huissen. Heavy freight traffic causes nuisance, danger, and damage to the living environment. Wherever possible, through freight traffic should be banned and guided around the town center via appropriate routes.

207096.explanation: D66 values traffic safety and pleasant living. Whether a ring road is the right solution depends on its effects on livability, landscape, costs, and alternatives such as traffic measures within the town. This requires a careful consideration and study.

207097.explanation: A new ring road around Huissen is unfeasible, very expensive, and time-consuming. It does not offer a long-term solution to current problems. Moreover, there is already a functional ring road, the A325. The focus should be on immediately feasible measures to restrict through (freight) traffic.

207098.explanation: D66 wants to make cycling and walking more attractive and safer. More and better cycle paths enhance traffic safety, encourage healthy exercise, and provide a good alternative to cars, especially for short distances within and between towns.

207099.explanation: Everyone should be able to participate independently in society. By financially supporting entrepreneurs to make their buildings accessible, barriers for people with disabilities disappear, and shops, hospitality, and facilities become better accessible to all.

207100.explanation: Affordable housing is under pressure. Large rent increases especially affect people with low or middle incomes. The municipality must work to ensure affordable rents and engage in discussions with housing corporations so that housing remains accessible to everyone.

207101.explanation: Thanks to D66, Lingewaard is a rainbow municipality. Projects for LGBTQIA+ residents contribute to acceptance, safety, and equal opportunities. By supporting these initiatives, the municipality works towards an inclusive society where no one is excluded.

207103.explanation: Street coaches play an important role in prevention, addressing youth, and preventing nuisance. They are accessible and visible in the neighborhood and contribute to safety and pleasant living. Ending street coaches would weaken this preventive approach.

207104.explanation: Good information helps residents make healthier choices and prevents long-term health problems. By focusing on exercise, nutrition, and mental health, the municipality contributes to well-being and reduces pressure on care and support services.

207105.explanation: Daycare offers structure, meeting opportunities, and support for people who need care and relieves informal caregivers. Investing in this keeps care accessible, prevents more intensive care, and allows people to participate independently and pleasantly in society longer.

207044.explanation: Collaboration with other municipalities is necessary to maintain quality and benefit from knowledge and expertise. It also saves costs and personnel capacity.

207045.explanation: Recent years show that neighborhood platforms receive sufficient funds to contribute to a pleasant living environment. If in the future more money is needed, there is always room for discussion.

207046.explanation: It is wonderful when people take joint responsibility for their living environment. It also strengthens social cohesion among neighbors.

207047.explanation: Our tourist tax is comparable to neighboring municipalities. It is important that this money is spent on good facilities for tourists.

207049.explanation: There is high demand from businesses to establish themselves in Lingewaard, but the business parks are full. We prefer to invest money to become an even more business-friendly municipality.

207050.explanation: The current sites are full. It is important to offer expansion options to existing local businesses in Lingewaard. However, we do not want to accommodate even more logistics companies in our municipality.

207051.explanation: After all, we must also keep an eye on the livability of residents living nearby.

207053.explanation: Our farmers must have enough space to continue operating their businesses. However, sometimes it is necessary to engage in nature compensation when building new housing.

207054.explanation: The municipality commits through the use of civil service capacity and the provision of knowledge. It is important that this remains possible in the future.

207055.explanation: It is important to encourage businesses to work as sustainably as possible.

207056.explanation: The Energy Desk has an important and accessible function to inform and support residents as well as possible regarding sustainability issues.

207057.explanation: The polluter pays. If waste can be disposed of for free, taxes such as property tax (OZB) will have to rise to cover the costs.

207059.explanation: From a sustainability perspective, it is important not to mow too often. However, in case of potentially unsafe traffic situations, certain sections should be mowed more frequently.

207061.explanation: Yes, first utilize all other possibilities such as installing solar panels on roofs as much as possible.

207063.explanation: We need to make progress in sustainability, and we must all contribute. This requires effort from residents, businesses, and governments.

207064.explanation: It is important that affordability, quality, and service of recycling centers improve. It is secondary whether this is a private recycling center or not.

207065.explanation: Reward good behavior and make polluters pay. The more waste you separate and prevent, the fewer costs you incur.

207066.explanation: We have more than enough resources to work on the integration of newcomers.

207067.explanation: It is important that migrant workers have good and safe housing facilities. And that they do not lose their housing immediately when they lose their jobs.

207068.explanation: We have more than enough resources to work on the integration of newcomers.

207070.explanation: If possible, focus on small-scale shelter locations.

207071.explanation: Disagree, but support swimming lessons for children in low-income families and more funding for sports and exercise at schools.

207072.explanation: Through associations in Lingewaard and more music lessons at schools.

207074.explanation: We already have a fantastic hockey club in Lingewaard. However, we want to make hockey possible for children in Huissen.

207075.explanation: There is less police capacity available. Therefore, it is important that the municipality takes responsibility for safety in Lingewaard.

207076.explanation: Only where it is really necessary and adds value. Keep an eye on the privacy of our residents. If it is not needed, then preferably not.

207077.explanation: BOAs (special enforcement officers) must have good equipment.

207080.explanation: Important to support our young people (also financially) in finding their first home to buy.

207081.explanation: This matches the needs of our residents and housing seekers in Lingewaard.

207082.explanation: Depending on the situation, apartment complexes can be higher than four floors if beautifully and greenly integrated. It must fit the environment and the character of the town.

207083.explanation: Important for livability in the towns and for our housing seekers in Lingewaard. We do not build for speculators.

207085.explanation: It is important that we have sufficient accessible green spaces in every center of our municipality.

207086.explanation: We must explore the possibilities of nuclear energy with safety as a key principle. Nuclear energy is indispensable to move away from polluting energy production.

207087.explanation: Everyone must be able to participate. Besides financial support, it is important to help people become self-sufficient again.

207088.explanation: We want to help more people find work and allocate funds for this. We believe more in guidance towards regular jobs than in creating subsidized jobs.

207089.explanation: It is important for the municipality to contribute to poverty reduction. From a humanitarian perspective, but also financially. Prevention avoids bigger problems and costs.

207090.explanation: It is good for the municipality to fight energy poverty. By insulating homes, we help this group maintain permanently low monthly expenses.

207091.explanation: We ensure that the supply of charging stations keeps pace with demand. But this must not come at the expense of parking options for non-electric drivers.

207092.explanation: The centers of our communities must remain easily accessible to visitors, and sufficient parking facilities are an important part of that.

207093.explanation: Line 33 connects all our communities in Lingewaard. We do support the promotion and assistance of volunteer transport.

207094.explanation: It is important that pedestrians and cyclists can travel safely over the dikes.

207095.explanation: We want to limit truck traffic in Huissen to destination traffic only. The nuisance from heavy truck traffic in Huissen is a thorn in our side.

207096.explanation: With further expansion of the number of homes in the Haalderen and Gendt centers, new access routes will need to be created. This does not necessarily have to be a ring road.

207097.explanation: With further expansion of the number of homes in the Huissen and Angeren centers, new access routes will need to be created. This does not necessarily have to be a ring road.

207098.explanation: We find it important to encourage cycling. It’s not only about more bike paths, but also about widening and improving them.

207099.explanation: As a municipality, we take a leading role in making our own municipal facilities accessible. Entrepreneurs have their own responsibilities in this. We can support them with this.

207100.explanation: In recent years it has become clear that our housing corporations implement only minor rent increases that are necessary for sustainability and new construction of social rental housing.

207101.explanation: The same conditions apply to all subsidies, regardless of sexual orientation. We stand for an inclusive society.

207103.explanation: It is good to stay in contact with young people and to detect and intervene early when problems arise.

207104.explanation: Providing information contributes to healthy and happy aging. Through prevention, we avoid additional pressure on healthcare.

207105.explanation: It is important to relieve informal caregivers. People need to stay in their homes longer, which requires facilities such as day care and guidance for, for example, people with dementia.

207044.explanation: The SP supports cooperation with other municipalities when the benefits outweigh the disadvantages. However, this is not the case with every collaboration.

207045.explanation: For many years it has been policy that neighborhood platforms have a budget that can be invested in consultation with the municipality. As far as the SP is concerned, that will remain so. An inflation adjustment is appropriate.

207046.explanation: Maintenance of public green spaces requires expertise and is primarily the municipality's task. If volunteers want to help the municipality with simple neighborhood green maintenance, this is possible in consultation.

207047.explanation: The current amount of €1.25 per night at a Lingewaard campsite is lower than in most surrounding municipalities. It is a very reasonable amount that does not need to be changed.

207049.explanation: That depends on the amount. If it is a small sum and there is a good chance it will pay for itself, the SP can accept it; otherwise not.

207050.explanation: Lingewaard currently has ample space reserved for businesses. The remaining available land, according to the SP, is intended for housing, agriculture, and nature.

207051.explanation: To prevent nuisance, cafes and restaurants should not close too late.

207053.explanation: The SP wants to strengthen green spaces both inside and outside the centers. It would be wonderful if agricultural lands located in the floodplains were converted into beautiful nature areas.

207054.explanation: Currently, there is no financial support for this purpose from the municipal fund. These residents can apply for subsidy funds from the national government via the Lingewaard Energy Desk. The SP is in favor of keeping it that way.

207055.explanation: The SP welcomes companies that operate fully sustainably, but also understands companies that are not yet fully sustainable. After all, we are in the middle of the transition and consider employment very important.

207056.explanation: Sustainably upgrading the home is not only good for the environment but also for residents’ wallets.

207057.explanation: The SP prefers Lingewaard to adopt a system where each household annually receives an 'exempt weight' of 200 kg for bulky waste and mattresses. Above this annual allowance, delivering such waste at a recycling center costs 25 cents per kilo. Delivery of green waste, paper, glass, and metals remains free.

207059.explanation: Mowing more often not only costs more money but also adversely affects biodiversity. The SP wants to keep the current number of mowings unchanged for the coming years.

207061.explanation: Solar panels do not belong on fertile agricultural land or in nature but on the roofs of homes and businesses!

207063.explanation: The SP supports a municipal heating company because it better controls costs for residents.

207064.explanation: A municipal recycling center has many advantages, such as better service and lower prices. The SP supports a municipal recycling center. However, private recycling centers should remain open, according to the SP.

207065.explanation: This discourages waste separation and leads to a significant increase in waste disposal charges.

207066.explanation: The municipality receives integration funds from the national government for this purpose. The available budget meets the needs.

207067.explanation: Poor working conditions, exploitation, and poor living conditions must be prevented.

207068.explanation: Both status holders and the community benefit from a quick transition from benefits to paid employment. This effort pays for itself quickly.

207070.explanation: That depends on the number of asylum seekers Lingewaard is obliged to accommodate by the national government. If the number is low, it is not necessary; otherwise, it is.

207071.explanation: School swimming is good for water safety, helps children enjoy earning a diploma and maintain skills, which contributes to their motor development, health, and self-reliance in the water.

207072.explanation: In principle, parents should pay for music lessons themselves. However, it must be prevented that children do not receive music lessons because their parents cannot afford it. In other words, municipal support is provided to families with a low income (via the Participation Scheme), otherwise not.

207074.explanation: Sport is important for body and mind. The SP is keen to promote more sports in Lingewaard. If there is sufficient interest for a hockey club in Huissen, the SP is positive about constructing the necessary hockey fields.

207075.explanation: Since Lingewaard will continue to expand in the coming years and the available police capacity is lagging, it is good to have more municipal enforcement officers who focus on safety.

207076.explanation: Agreed, but only at certain locations where serious nuisance and/or crime occurs systematically or is expected.

207077.explanation: Bodycams accurately record what happened. This can help resolve disputes and provide clarity in cases with conflicting evidence or statements. The presence of a camera also works preventively for the safety of the enforcement officers.

207080.explanation: The municipality of Lingewaard also provides starter loans. However, the available amount is too low, limiting their use.

207081.explanation: In plans for new residential areas, the SP aims for 30% 'affordable rental,' 10% 'mid-range rental,' 35% 'affordable ownership,' and 25% free sector housing.

207082.explanation: According to the SP, a maximum of seven stories may be built in Huissen and Bemmel as long as this fits well into the surroundings. In the smaller villages, the maximum remains five stories, provided it is well integrated.

207083.explanation: Thanks to a proposal submitted by the SP, investors can no longer snatch newly built owner-occupied homes in the so-called 'affordable price range' from under the noses of starters. This 'self-occupancy obligation' proposal makes an exception for people who want to buy a home for family members.

207085.explanation: The SP aims for as much greenery as possible in every new residential area plan. This includes many fields with lots of trees, hedges, and shrubs.

207086.explanation: The SP believes Lingewaard should only consider sustainability options that are truly sustainable and can be realized in the short term. The SP mainly focuses on properly insulating homes and solar energy on the roofs.

207087.explanation: Driving residents further into poverty is not only antisocial, it ultimately costs society much more than giving people with low income a helping hand.

207088.explanation: These jobs provide a stable income, social security, social participation, and help reduce the distance to the regular labor market. It lowers the number of people dependent on benefits and enables more people to contribute to the economy.

207089.explanation: Stopping debt forgiveness not only leads to more problems for low-income households but also results in much higher costs for society.

207090.explanation: Housing corporations should ensure proper insulation of rental homes. Residents with low income who own their homes should, according to the SP, receive support for insulating their homes.

207091.explanation: At this moment, the number of parking spaces that serve as charging points is sufficient.

207092.explanation: The SP is open to a limited reduction of the blue zones.

207093.explanation: Unlike all other parties, the SP did not agree to cuts in AVAN transport. According to the SP, Lingewaard should not reduce spending on this transportation in the coming years.

207094.explanation: The SP supports measures that make the dike roads safer but considers setting the entire dike to a maximum speed of 30 km/h for car traffic to be excessive.

207095.explanation: To relieve the enormous pressure on Huissen’s main traffic artery, the SP has long advocated banning through heavy truck traffic on this road. Destination truck traffic needed for picking up or delivering goods may, of course, continue to drive through Huissen.

207096.explanation: If politics choose large-scale housing development near Haalderen, a ring or bypass road is necessary for good traffic flow.

207097.explanation: Experts who studied the 'Huissen bypass plan' conclude that the plan will cost taxpayers about 100 million euros and will lead to significant nuisance and danger in Angeren. In short, it is an exorbitantly expensive unwise plan that will never be realized.

207098.explanation: The better the bicycle path network, the more attractive it is to choose the bike instead of the car. Cycling is good for body, mind, and the environment, and it also reduces traffic pressure on the roads.

207099.explanation: According to the SP, all buildings should be accessible to people with disabilities; however, unlike public buildings, companies themselves are responsible for being accessible to people with disabilities and for the associated costs.

207100.explanation: Given the excellent financial situation of Waardwonen and the good condition of the housing stock, substantial rent increases are not needed to build, maintain, and make sustainable improvements. Yet, other parties voted against the SP proposal opposing the large rent increases by Waardwonen.

207101.explanation: When the municipality is asked to support LGBTQIA+ projects aimed at helping this group feel more accepted and safe, the municipality should respond positively.

207103.explanation: Street coaches act as a link between young people and the municipality, enforcement officers, and police. Deploying street coaches not only reduces youth nuisance and minor crime but also helps young people to prevent worse problems.

207104.explanation: Research shows that youth in Lingewaard consume more alcohol than in other municipalities. This calls for more educational campaigns by the municipality in cooperation with schools.

207105.explanation: People with a low income should also be able to use day care. With the SP at the helm, recent cuts in care that all other parties agreed to will be reversed and sufficient investment will be made in care to ensure it is accessible to everyone.

207044.explanation: The municipality of Lingewaard should focus on what happens within its own communities. By organizing more locally without unnecessary collaborations, costs can be saved. Additionally, we ensure that property tax income from Lingewaard is also spent in Lingewaard.

207045.explanation: Neighborhood platforms have extensive knowledge of their specific district. This enables them to make better decisions about creating a pleasant living environment.

207046.explanation: Residents often want their neighborhood to look neat and well-maintained. If residents can maintain the greenery in their area themselves, it will be maintained more often. This ultimately saves costs and improves green maintenance.

207047.explanation: The tourist tax is used to promote the municipality as a tourist attraction. However, Lingewaard has few major tourist attractions or activities. Therefore, advertising for this and increasing the tax is unnecessary.

207049.explanation: When a company wants to establish itself in the municipality of Lingewaard, it weighs various factors such as costs, facilities, and location. A promotional campaign contributes little to this.

207050.explanation: Currently, there are enough industrial estates in Lingewaard.

207051.explanation: As long as local residents do not experience nuisance from extended opening hours, cafés and restaurants should be able to decide their own opening times.

207053.explanation: There is already a lot of nature in Lingewaard. Therefore, adding more nature is unnecessary. Moreover, agricultural land is very important for Lingewaard farmers and for the Dutch food supply.

207054.explanation: Sustainable energy is often unstable and, in the case of wind energy, literally depends on the wind. Furthermore, sustainable energy is often more expensive than regular energy. When residents want to collectively produce sustainable energy, they should pay for this themselves.

207055.explanation: The municipality must consider potential health risks to the surrounding area when new companies establish themselves. As long as the health of nearby residents is not at risk, a company should be able to settle in Lingewaard. Whether the company wants to operate sustainably is its own choice.

207056.explanation: The Lingewaard Energy Desk provides a lot of advice on making homes more sustainable. However, companies can do this as well. When a resident wants to insulate their home, they can request a quote from a specialized company.

207057.explanation: Collecting and processing waste involves costs. If disposing of waste were free, property tax (ozb) would increase. To avoid this, a cost-covering waste levy must be implemented.

207059.explanation: Currently, there is a lot of overdue maintenance on municipal grass fields and verges. To make Lingewaard look well-maintained, these should be mowed more frequently.

207061.explanation: Agricultural land is essential for food supply in the Netherlands. Moreover, solar panels are less consistent than other energy sources.

207063.explanation: A homeowner should be able to decide how to heat their house. With a heat network, they are tied to a single supplier, which can lead to higher costs. Additionally, heat networks are more vulnerable to malfunctions than other heating methods.

207064.explanation: When there are multiple recycling centers in Lingewaard, competition arises, making it cheaper for residents to bring their waste. Moreover, residents often live closer to one of the recycling centers due to multiple locations.

207065.explanation: Waste levies must be cost-covering. Those who produce a lot of waste pay more than those who produce less. This prevents residents from unnecessarily paying too much.

207066.explanation: The municipality must stop accommodating asylum seekers and status holders. This costs a lot of money and brings little benefit to residents. Moreover, if no asylum seekers and status holders live in Lingewaard, it is unnecessary to spend money on integration.

207067.explanation: Labor migrants come to the Netherlands voluntarily and are responsible for their own housing. If they receive housing from an employer, they agree to this voluntarily. Those who are not satisfied can look for another employer.

207068.explanation: When status holders come to the Netherlands, they cost taxpayers a lot of money. When they have a paid job, they can repay these costs in the form of taxes.

207070.explanation: The municipality must guarantee that no new asylum seeker centers are built and prevent the opening of the asylum seeker center in Bemmel.

207071.explanation: Education is funded by the Ministry of Education, Culture and Science (OCW). If schools want to reintroduce swimming lessons, this must be financed from The Hague.

207072.explanation: Funding personal activities of children is not a municipal task. Moreover, taxes would have to increase further to finance music lessons for children.

207074.explanation: When a new hockey club wants to install a hockey field, they must pay for it themselves. Furthermore, it is more efficient for sports clubs to share facilities. This lowers costs and ensures better maintenance of the facilities.

207075.explanation: The safety of residents in Lingewaard is important. In recent years, crime in the Netherlands has increased, meaning the police do not always have capacity for minor issues. Community officers (boa’s) do have time to resolve small-scale incidents and increase visibility.

207076.explanation: Camera surveillance is an effective way to monitor locations with a lot of nuisance or crime. However, cameras should not be placed just anywhere because they can significantly infringe on residents' privacy.

207077.explanation: Bodycams can be used to prevent violence against community officers (boa’s). In addition, the footage can serve as evidence in court.

207080.explanation: By building more in suitable locations, house prices will naturally decrease. Providing loans to young people is not the municipality’s task but the banks'.

207081.explanation: There is currently a high demand for social rental housing. To meet this demand, more social rental homes must be built. At the same time, it must be prevented that too many social rental homes are built, so the shortage in the regular housing market does not increase.

207082.explanation: High-rise flats do not fit in every village. Therefore, it should be assessed per plan whether a flat building of more than four floors is suitable at that location, instead of applying a fixed guideline.

207083.explanation: When a homeowner is required to live in their home, they cannot rent it out. This makes investing in Lingewaard homes unattractive for investors, which means project developers are less inclined to build new homes.
